headline: 11-Year-Old's Quick Action Saves Drowning Man at Pool
meta_description: Young swimmer in Kentucky springs into action when no adults respond, diving in to rescue an unconscious man from a swimming pool.
summary_short: An 11-year-old boy in Kentucky saw a man unconscious in a pool and didn't hesitate—he dove in and saved his life when no one else acted.
body:
When an adult fell unconscious at an apartment swimming pool in Kentucky, it took an 11-year-old to see what needed to be done. Young Avory Woolery noticed the man in distress and recognized that bystanders around him weren't stepping in. Rather than wait for someone older, he made the decision to act.

Without hesitation, Woolery jumped into the pool and pulled the man to safety. His quick thinking and courage made the difference between a tragedy and a rescue. The incident is a reminder that heroism isn't determined by age or size—it's determined by the willingness to help when it matters most.

Woolery's actions have earned him recognition in his community. His parents, and the man he rescued, likely remain grateful for a young person who chose to be brave when bravery was needed.
